scope.target: Profile, deletion, and technology-selection rule ownership
constraints: NSubstitute is the default profile selection with one explicit target override
Use one generic technology-selection override shape for mocking, ORM, broker, and similar adapters
Soft delete is a profile-default Aggregate Repository behavior with explicit target opt-out
Physical purge requires a dedicated command/use case and restricted capability
non_goals: Forbid a target-owned mock framework override

steps: Align NSubstitute canonical and derived consumers
Make soft-delete repository semantics profile-default with explicit opt-out
Update DELETE-SOFT-001 ownership strength and applicability
execution.last_completed_step: Generic technology selection, NSubstitute default/override, and soft-delete profile semantics were aligned and validated.
execution.next_action: Proceed to EXAMPLE-001; independent verification remains in VERIFY-001.

results.summary: Added TECH-SELECT-001 and TEST-MOCK-001, introduced a target-owned generic selection schema, made NSubstitute an overridable profile default, and changed DELETE-SOFT-001 to profile-default with explicit target opt-out while keeping purge separate.
tests_run: AI context validator passed with 10 owned rules
Workflow, assessment, and shell-asset validators passed
Mandatory NSubstitute/Moq and unconditional soft-delete scans returned no matches
results.residual_risk: Independent verification is pending and MIGRATE-001 must finish disposition coverage for all derived consumers before ROUTE-001.
